utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with required details:
- health_coverage: "physical and mental health insurance with costs" — Plan named, cost given (165), and details mention comprehensive physical and mental health coverage. Fully specified.
- property_coverage: "home/contents insurance with costs" — Plan named, cost given (35), and details indicate home office/contents coverage for equipment and liability. Fully specified.
- travel_coverage: "travel insurance with costs" — Plan named, cost given (115), and details include emergency medical evacuation and extreme sports. Fully specified.
- additional_services: "any enhancement services with costs" — Provided as null. This item is present but does not include services or costs; not fully specified per requirement of including any with costs. Since the format requires it to contain services if any, null means it is not specified with costs, so not fully specified.
- total_budget: "complete monthly/annual cost breakdown" — Provided as a single number 315 (monthly) without an annual breakdown. The requirement asks for complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; annual is missing, so not fully specified.
Thus, 3 items are fully specified (health, property, travel) out of 5 total.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), Home Office Protection (property enhancement), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel). I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings. LifeFirst Standard Plan appears under primary_health_coverage -> physical_health with rating 9.
